title: carex dioica var. custoriana
intro: Carex dioica var. custoriana, also known as the tufted sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, characterized by its distinctive, cylindrical spikes of small, brownish flowers. Traditionally, the roots and rhizomes of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Carex dioica var. custoriana are said to include relief from urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and urinary incontinence, as well as potential antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as an infusion or tincture, although further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of its uses.
